reforms.description: The 1978 budget introduced a modified three-year scheme providing a lower corporation tax rate of 25% for firms that increased employment, alongside allowing free depreciation of new plant and machinery as a normal feature of capital allowance arrangements, enhancing investment incentives.
reforms.component_name: Tax Incentives for Employment and Investment
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures incentivize firms to invest and hire, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures were part of the 1978 budget and are stated to be implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While not a structural change, these measures significantly enhance the investment climate for firms.
reforms.source_quote: The three year scheme... was modified. As originally introduced the lower rate of tax was available to firms who increased employment and the volume of sales by 3 per cent and 5 per cent per annum respectively. In the 1978 budget the condition requiring an increase in the volume of sales was dropped.

reforms.description: The Government announced a commitment of £4.6 million to Ireland from the European Regional Development Fund, aimed at supporting regional development initiatives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This funding supports regional development, which can enhance economic activity and growth in targeted areas.
reforms.status_evidence: The commitment was announced and is stated to be implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The funding represents a significant investment in regional development, though not a systemic overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Government Information Services announced a further commitment of £4.6 million to Ireland from the European Regional Development Fund.

reforms.description: Ireland is set to receive £9.4 million from the European Social Fund to support vocational training activities, enhancing the skills of the workforce.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in vocational training enhances workforce skills, which can lead to improved productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The funding commitment is stated to be implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The funding for vocational training is significant for workforce development but does not represent a major systemic change.
reforms.source_quote: It was announced that Ireland is to receive £9.4 million from the European Social Fund following the Commission's approval of the first series of 1977 applications for assistance from the Fund for vocational training activities.
